$comment: Page contract for /demo-maui-3d.html. The basemap, exclusive bases, glyphs, and the terrarium DEM come from the SHARED demo contract (assets/demo/layers.json) so the 3D page can never drift from the seeded server — this file declares only what is specific to this page: the maui-buildings layer, the bundled offline fixture, and the 2.5D scene framing. maui-buildings seeded 2026-06-12 from Overture Maps release 2026-05-20.0 (theme=buildings/type=building, Maui Nui bbox -157.40,20.45,-155.95,21.25), license ODbL — attribution 'Overture Maps Foundation · © OpenStreetMap contributors' is REQUIRED and rendered on the map's attribution control.

map.$comment: Boot view = first scene (Kahului Harbor) framing, applied instantly when the buildings lane is live; falls back to a wider flat view while probing fails.

buildings.name: Buildings (Overture)

pmtiles.$comment: PRIMARY SOURCE (seeded 2026-06-12): pre-baked MVT pyramid (tippecanoe -l layer, z12-14, --drop-densest-as-needed, attrs name/class/subtype/height/num_floors/render_height/height_source, 582 tiles, 2.7 MB) from the same Overture FlatGeobuf that seeded the live layer (42,674 footprints). Uploaded to s3://honua-demo-data-585192672263/maui-buildings-static (ContentType application/vnd.pmtiles, metadata operation=publish). maui-3d.js prefers this block when the archive HEAD-probes OK; the dynamic OGC Tiles route below stays seeded as the documented live-rendering fallback. sourceLayer 'layer' matches the server's ST_AsMVT convention.

tiles.$comment: Source maxzoom 14: buildings need real z14 geometry for clean extrusion edges; MapLibre overzooms past 14. minzoom 12 keeps county-wide views off the extrusion path entirely (and off the database).

buildings.$renderHeightComment: render_height is computed at seed time: measured Overture height where present, else num_floors x 3 m, else 4 m default. height_source records which fallback fired.

fixture.$comment: Offline lane: ~200 real Kahului-harbor footprints cut from the same Overture extract that seeded the live layer. Same attributes, same extrusion primitive — only the source type differs (geojson vs vector tiles).

terrain.$comment: Elevation source values mirror the shared contract's terrain layer (maui-terrain-static, terrarium PMTiles via the range proxy). The page re-reads proxyUrl/encoding from layers.json at boot; these are documentation defaults.
